name: Dennis Mackrel
abstract: Dennis Mackrel (born April 3, 1962) is an American jazz drummer, composer, and arranger who was a member of the Count Basie Orchestra and the Vanguard Jazz Orchestra.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Nancy Wilson, Singer, and Joe Williams, Singer, with The Count Basie Orchestra
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: The Classic Song
George Shearing's 80th: Celebrate with a Jazz Legend "Lullaby of Birdland"
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

Carnegie Hall Notables: The Ninth Annual Notable Occasion: Rossano Sportiello and His American Jazz Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Clay Jenkins
about_quotes.evidence: He and Jeff Hamilton and Dennis Mackrel and Steve Houghton, and all these great drummers I’ve gotten to play with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clay Jenkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 18, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: I did some gigs years ago, Warren Vache, Michael Hashim on saxophone, James Chirillo the guitarist, Chip Jackson on bass and Dennis Mackrel who was here with the Dizzy band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: This time will be Terrell Stafford, Antonio Hart, I think Renee Rosnes, John Lee and Dennis Mackrel. And they’re giving us the best cabins on the boat.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: Dennis Mackrel has done two things for me, for this next gig... I just explain to them what I have in mind and they know, pretty well, what to do.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: we add drums, either Bernard Purdie or Dennis Mackrel or Joe Cocuzzo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: Dennis Mackrel wrote a wonderful chart for us on “In a Mellow Tone,”
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001
